The '''future tense''' in Aelaki is the generic future frame, referring to events expected to occur beyond tomorrow. Events happening later today use the [[Hodiernal|hodiernal]], and events expected tomorrow use the [[Crastinal|crastinal]].

== Formation ==

The future tense shares its evidential and modal suffixes with the [[Crastinal|crastinal]] but lacks the '''goki-''' prefix that marks "tomorrow" specifically.

== Evidential and modal suffixes ==

{| class="wikitable"
! Category !! Suffix !! Meaning
|-
| Auditory/Reported || '''-rum''' || The speaker has heard or been told the event will happen
|-
| Inferential || '''-mu''' || The speaker infers the event will happen
|-
| Optative || '''-ya''' || The speaker wishes or hopes the event will happen
|-
| Deliberative || '''-yam''' || The speaker is considering or deliberating whether the event should happen
|}

== Diachronic notes ==

The reported future suffix '''-rum''' is historically derived from '''-ro''' (the [[Past|past]] auditory/hearsay marker) fused with '''-mu''' (inferential), reflecting an origin where future reports were conceptualized as "hearsay about what is inferred."

== See also ==

* [[Crastinal]] -- "tomorrow"
* [[Hodiernal]] -- "today/later today"
* [[Past]]
* [[Present]]
* [[Hesternal]]
* [[Mythic]]

[[Category:grammatical tenses]]
[[Category:git synced pages]]
